title: Mission-Style Dining Table
summary: A four-square dining table with 4x4 legs, jigsaw-cut diamond slats between them, and layered molding — mission looks without mortise-and-tenon joinery.
description:
This gets the heavy, grounded look of a mission dining table using dimensional lumber and pocket screws instead of traditional joinery. The legs are solid 4x4s, and the character comes from two places: slat panels between the legs with small diamonds cut through them, and layered base-and-screen molding around the feet that reads as furniture-grade trim. It's a lot of small decorative cuts rather than difficult joints, so patience matters more than skill here.

The top is a plywood panel wrapped in a mitered 1x2 frame, with a second mitered 1x2 apron set inside the edges. Cut those miters carefully — 45s that don't close leave gaps at every corner of the top, which is exactly where the eye lands. Fill, sand, and it disappears.

The diamonds in the 1x4 slats are drawn 3" from top and bottom, cut with a jigsaw, and sanded smooth; laying them out consistently across all six slats is what keeps the base from looking hand-cut. There's also a nice finishing trick baked in: the small pieces of screen molding along the bottom rail exist to hide screw heads, so drive your structural screws where that molding will cover them.
timeLabel: 8–12 hrs across a couple of sessions with finishing

tools.note: The base and top frame go together with pocket screws.
Clean 45s on the top frame and apron molding are the whole finished look.
Cuts the diamond cutouts in the slats.
Attaches the top frame and molding with glue.
Check the base level as you attach each leg unit.

materials.name: 1x4 board, 8 ft
materials.note: Diamond slats and top cross supports.

materials.name: Stain and polyurethane
materials.note: 3–6 coats of poly if you do a painted-graphic top.

cutList.note: 45° mitered ends, flush with the top.
Mitered, set inside the top frame.
Mitered around the bottom of each leg.
One inch above the bottom rail.
Cover screw heads along the bottom rail.

steps.title: Frame and apron the top
steps.body: Miter the 57 1/2" and 27 1/2" 1x2s and glue-and-nail them around the plywood panel, flush with the top face, to frame it. Then miter the 56" and 26" 1x2s and set them just inside the frame edges to form an apron under the lip. Fill the nail holes and any miter gaps with putty and sand smooth — the corners are where the eye goes.

steps.title: Cut the diamond slats
steps.body: Lay out 2" diamonds 3" from the top and bottom of the 1x4 slats, positioned to sit between the slats as shown. Cut them with a jigsaw and sand the openings smooth. Do the layout identically on all six or the base looks hand-cut.

steps.title: Build the two leg units
steps.body: Assemble each pair of legs with the 2x4 side rails and the slat panel between them. Using the pocket-hole jig, fasten the rails 5" from the bottom with two 2-1/2" screws each. Check each unit for level and square before moving to the second.

steps.title: Join the base with the stretcher
steps.body: Connect the two leg units with the 49 1/2" center stretcher, centered on the lower 2x4s and glued and screwed with 2-1/2" screws from the outside. Add the 1x4 cross supports across the top of the base, centered, to carry the top. Check level again — the floor finds every error now.

steps.title: Add the decorative molding
steps.body: Miter the base molding around the bottom of each leg and glue-and-nail it on. An inch above the bottom rail, run the mitered screen molding around. Finally, tuck the 3" screen molding pieces over the structural screw heads on the bottom rail so nothing shows. Putty, sand, and you're ready to finish.

steps.title: Finish and mount the top
steps.body: Prime and paint, or stain the whole piece — the source table even projects a graphic onto the top, traces it, and paints it before staining and topcoating with several coats of poly. When the finish is cured, set the top on the base and screw up from underneath through the cross supports with 1-1/4" screws.

images.alt: Mission-style dining table with 4x4 legs and diamond-cut slat panels
